title: CVE-2026-50005: Brickcom Cameras Default Credentials
summary: [P1] Brickcom cameras ship with default credentials, allowing any unauthenticated user to access the camera. No patch is available, and exploitation can lead to unauthorized access to the camera's live feed. Why now: Default credentials increase the risk of exploitation, and the lack of a patch makes it a high-priority issue. (confidence: 0.90)
classification_reasoning: The default credentials allow unauthorized access to the camera's live feed, which aligns with the credential_compromise problem type.

title: CVE-2026-53807: OpenClaw Auth Bypass
summary: [P1] OpenClaw before 2026.5.6 contains an authorization bypass vulnerability in Teleg, with no patch available. This vulnerability can be exploited to gain unauthorized access to the system. Why now: Lack of patch availability increases the risk of exploitation. (confidence: 0.80)
classification_reasoning: The vulnerability allows unauthorized access to the system, which aligns with the auth_bypass problem type.
